title: Velvety Smoked Dutch Rice Pilaf
description: This Dutch-inspired pilaf combines tender rice with smoky flavors and anti-inflammatory spices, finished with a rich wine reduction.
instructions: Heat the binchotan charcoal in a large Dutch oven over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smoky temperature.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until fragrant.
Add the rice to the pot and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is lightly toasted.
Add the chicken broth, white wine, apple cider vinegar, turmeric, cumin, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 18-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve immediately.
